About.title: About
About.subtitle: A bit about myself.
About.aboutP1: I’m a developer based in Aarhus, Denmark with a background in computer science and a strong interest in building well-structured web applications. I enjoy working across the stack, but especially crafting responsive and maintainable frontends with React and TypeScript.
About.aboutP2: My journey into development started during my Datamatiker education, where I focused heavily on backend development, APIs, and software architecture. Since then I’ve continued building projects and exploring modern web technologies while studying for a Bachelor’s degree in Web Development.
About.aboutP3: I enjoy turning ideas into working products, learning new technologies, and solving real problems through code. When I’m not coding, you’ll probably find me gaming, watching football, or experimenting with new side projects.

Projects.title: Projects
Projects.subtitle: Personal projects I've been working on.

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.subtitle: Email, LinkedIn, carrier pigeon...

WorkExperience.title: Work Experience
WorkExperience.subtitle: My career as a Software Developer.
WorkExperience.intro: I'm at the start of my career, building momentum through education and hands-on projects. Here's where I've been learning and shipping.

bachelor.org: Copenhagen School of Design and Technology (KEA)
bachelor.description: Currently studying for a Bachelor's in Web Development, going deeper into modern frontend architecture, accessibility, and building production-ready web apps.

datamatiker.description: Completed a two-year program focused on backend development, APIs, databases, and software architecture. Built several full-stack projects and a bachelor semester project with a team.

amori.title: Amori
amori.description: Journal for tracking relationship milestones and memories.
amori.longAbout: A private journal-style web app for tracking relationship milestones, memories, and day-to-day moments. Built to stay fast and easy to return to, with a clear focus on content over chrome.
features: Entries for special dates and shared memories
Simple, calm UI that stays out of the way
Typed, maintainable client with React and TypeScript

quartermark.title: QuarterMark
quartermark.description: Jackbox-style party game for New Year's.
quartermark.longAbout: A Realtime party game in the style of a Jackbox room: one host screen and players join on their phones. Made for high-energy New Year's sessions with C# and SignalR for sync between clients.
features: Room code join flow for many players at once
Web UI on React, backend on .NET
Designed for a single shared screen and mobile controllers

yeetcraft.title: Yeetcraft
yeetcraft.description: Leaderboard tracker for friendly banter - tracks mistakes across a friend group.
yeetcraft.longAbout: A small-group leaderboard for tracking in-game and in-joke 'mistakes' with friends, built for laughs first and long-running rivalries second. The stack mixes Kotlin, React, and TanStack for a snappy, typed experience end to end.
features: Web UI in React and TypeScript
TanStack Query for data fetching and cache discipline

lineup.title: LineUp
lineup.description: Co-developed a social + marketplace platform for freelance musicians to showcase skills and get hired. Also used as a bachelor semester project.
lineup.longAbout: A social and marketplace product for freelance musicians: profiles, discovery, and booking-style flows. Co-developed and used as a bachelor semester project, with a modern stack and realtime where it matters for messaging and live updates.
features: Profile and discovery for musicians and bookers
Supabase-backed data and Realtime for modern UX

memoir.title: Memoir
memoir.longAbout: A work-in-progress idea for a rich reading and personal library experience on the web. The goal is a calm, focused surface for long-form text with room to grow as the concept firms up.
features: WIP: core reading flow in progress
Placeholder content while the design is explored
